text:
Jump Festa (ジャンプフェスタ, Janpu Fesuta) is a 2-day anime and manga convention organized by Shueisha and held every December at Makuhari Messe, Tokyo. The event is centered around the Jump magazines, such as Weekly Shonen Jump. It features new manga, anime, movies, games, and merchandise.

Overview

The annual Jump Festa held every December in Tokyo is organized by Shueisha, primarily to celebrate and promote its Shonen Jump manga series. It serves as a major platform for manga and anime announcements, exclusive merchandise, and fan events. Over the years, Hunter × Hunter has featured prominently at Jump Festa, with panels, promotional videos, merchandise releases, and occasional appearances by staff or messages from creator Yoshihiro Togashi. The event has often been the first place for fans to learn about new manga arcs, anime adaptations, or special projects related to the series, making it a key moment in the Hunter × Hunter fandom calendar.

Hunter x Hunter at Jump Festa

Early 2000s

From the very beginning of Jump Festa in 1999, Hunter × Hunter was present in the form of limited merchandise. By the year 2000, fans could already find Jump Festa-branded collectibles, such as posters, telephone cards, and event-exclusive prints. These items demonstrated that the series had a presence at the festival, even in its early days, primarily through merchandise rather than large-scale stage announcements.

Jump Fest 2012

When the 2011 television reboot of Hunter × Hunter launched, the series received official space at Jump Festa 2012. The anime's booth, run by VAP, its distributor, sold exclusive goods and hosted special recordings with voice actors. This marked the series' first major organized presence at Jump Festa, tied directly to the excitement around the brand-new anime.

Jump Fest 2013

The following year, Hunter × Hunter used Jump Festa 2013 to promote its first feature-length film, Phantom Rouge, which was scheduled for release in January 2013. The festival featured film-related merchandise, stage appearances with the anime's cast, and lottery tie-ins. This year's presence focused heavily on preparing fans for the series' big leap to the big screen.

Jump Fest 2014

At Jump Festa 2014, Hunter × Hunter took merchandising to another level. The booth sold exclusive, themed products, including Greed Island replica jewelry (rings and necklaces), T-shirts, and clear files. Fans could also take photos with standees and enjoy special displays. This year highlighted the series' ability to create premium, festival-only goods that were deeply connected to its storyline.

Mid 2010s

In the years that followed, Hunter × Hunter maintained a steady, if quieter, presence at Jump Festa. The series was represented through a variety of limited-edition products, including posters, postcards, and small collectibles. These ongoing appearances kept the series visible and active at the festival.

Jump Fest 2024

After some quieter years, Hunter × Hunter made a dramatic return to Jump Festa in December 2023. At the Bushiroad booth, the company revealed that it was developing a new fighting game based on the series, later titled Hunter × Hunter: NEN × IMPACT. This was one of the most significant announcements in years, showcasing Jump Festa as a stage for major franchise news.

Jump Fest 2025

The following year, Hunter × Hunter returned again, this time with a playable demo of the new fighting game at the Bushiroad booth. Fans could try the game firsthand and receive special badges. Additionally, Bandai Spirits exhibited unique merchandise prototypes, including novelty figures, and series creator Yoshihiro Togashi contributed artwork for the festival's artist display. This event showed how the series continued to evolve its Jump-Festa presence — not only with products, but also interactive experiences and creative contributions.

Merchandise

Games & Game Swag

Jump Festa has often been the stage for Hunter × Hunter game-related promotions. The most notable was the announcement of the fighting game Nen × Impact at Jump Festa 2024, followed by its first playable demo at Jump Festa 2025. Attendees who tested the demo received novelty pin badges and other exclusive freebies, making the booth a popular gathering spot during the event. Earlier years also saw smaller game tie-ins, such as trading card game promotions or demo tickets, which reinforced Jump Festa's role as a testing ground and celebration space for Hunter × Hunter games.

Replica Jewelry & Props

In 2014, Jump Festa offered some of the most unique Hunter × Hunter merchandise in the form of replica jewelry from the Greed Island arc. Festival-goers could purchase wearable reproductions of the “Blue Planet” gem and the “Paladin's Necklace,” both advertised specifically as Jump Festa–limited goods. These high-end replicas stood out among the typical small trinkets usually sold at the event, giving fans a rare chance to own detailed props modeled directly after the series' iconic items.

Clothes

Exclusive clothing has been a recurring attraction for Hunter × Hunter fans at Jump Festa. Limited-edition anime T-shirts, featuring characters like Gon and Netero, were available at events such as Jump Festa 2014. More recent festivals have also offered updated designs, including logo-based shirts tied directly to the series' ongoing popularity. These apparel items, sold at Jump Shop booths or festival stalls, combine everyday wear with fandom pride and often become highly sought-after collectibles after the event.

Printed Goods

Paper-based collectibles are a staple of Jump Festa, and Hunter × Hunter has consistently appeared in this category. Fans could purchase or receive exclusive posters, A4 clear files, postcards, and even “art coaster” prints, many of which were produced only for the festival. These items were often distributed at publisher or distributor booths and later circulated widely in secondary markets, where their “Jump Festa” labeling confirmed their origin. Affordable and lightweight, these goods were popular with casual visitors and collectors alike.

Small Novelty Goods

Jump Festa has also provided a steady stream of smaller novelty merchandise for Hunter × Hunter. Event-exclusive can-badges, blind-box keychains, and enamel pins have appeared repeatedly, often tied to booth activities or demo experiences. For example, Bushiroad's booth at Jump Festa 2025 rewarded demo players with collectible pins, whereas earlier events offered blind-pack keychains that were sold only during the festival. These low-cost, easy-to-carry items made them perfect souvenirs for attendees and remain common in collector resale markets.

Figures & Prototype Displays

For figure collectors, Jump Festa has been an important venue to preview and celebrate Hunter × Hunter sculpts. Major manufacturers, such as Bandai Spirits (Tamashii Nations), have displayed prototypes of action figures and statues, including upcoming S.H. Figuarts models, at the Jump Festa booths. Banpresto's Ichiban Kuji prize figures have also been showcased during festival seasons, often with painted prototypes or display samples available for viewing. These displays provided fans with an early glimpse of upcoming releases and generated excitement around the franchise's collectibles.

Lottery & Prize Campaigns (Ichiban Kuji)

Jump Festa frequently coincides with the rollout of Hunter × Hunter Ichiban Kuji campaigns, which are lottery-based prize lines produced by Banpresto. These campaigns include figures, posters, clear files, and other themed prizes. While the lotteries are not always exclusive to the festival, Jump Festa booths often display the prize sets or host early promotional activities. As a result, Ichiban Kuji goods have become closely associated with the franchise's festival presence, bridging mainstream retail with the exclusivity of event hype.

Media & Special Editions

Special media releases have also intersected with Jump Festa's merchandising calendar. For example, the Hunter × Hunter Huncyclopedia and various DVD releases had first-press editions promoted during December, aligning with Jump Festa's schedule. While not always branded as event exclusives, these media items were often displayed or marketed around festival booths, capitalizing on the heightened fan interest during the event. Collectors later recognized these first-press versions as part of the Jump Festa season's wave of limited goods.

Displays & Creator Contributions

Beyond merchandise, Jump Festa has featured striking visual displays and contributions from the creator of Hunter × Hunter. Life-size standees, wall panels, and exhibition art regularly featured the series, and Yoshihiro Togashi has provided signed shikishi illustrations for festival displays in recent years. These pieces were typically shown in the main exhibition halls rather than sold, though occasional reproductions or lottery giveaways made them available to fans. Such contributions gave Hunter × Hunter a strong artistic presence at the festival, reinforcing its cultural significance alongside merchandise sales.

Announcements

Throughout all of Jump Festa events, exclusive merchandise were revealed.
A special Huncyclopedia DVD was revealed at Jump Festa 2012, in December 2011.
Hunter × Hunter: Phantom Rouge was announced and had its event-exclusive merchandise sold during Jump Festa 2013, in December 2012.
Hunter × Hunter: The Last Mission was announced and promoted during Jump Festa 2014, in December 2013.
Hunter × Hunter: Nen × Impact was revealed at Jump Festa 2024, in December 2023.
Hunter × Hunter: Nen × Impact's first playable Demo appeared at Jump Festa 2025, in December 2024.
